messages.content: Set into tv show The Arrow universe. Ta-er al-Sahfer and Nysa Al ghul are in a relationship. Both find themselves interested in new member Al Mansi (previously known as Nicolas before joining the ranks). He is a shy, young man. His dedication to training is second to none and he is making progress faster than anyone before him. He is also Someone who should easily be forgotten. His unassuming nature make him someone nobody really would think as an assassin. Story start with Al Mansi in the training halls. He as progressed by leaps and bound and is on part with both Sarab and Nyssa in hand to hand combat with a style leaning more on hard style. He is now Working on his meteor hammer. Both Nyssa and Sarah propose to help him. Nyssa proposes to start training in long range weapons like the bow and arrow. Training session. Written like fiction book.No declaration of romantic interest between Nyssa, Sarah & Al Mansi. The beginning of a friendship.

Al Mansi stood in the training hall, his meteor hammer spinning fluidly around him. Sweat glistened on his forehead as he worked through the intricate movements he had been practicing for weeks. His progress had been remarkable, and he was proud of the effort he had put into his training.

As he finished his routine, he heard a soft clap from behind him. Turning, he saw Nyssa Al Ghul standing there, a look of admiration on her face. "Impressive," she said, noting the way his weapon moved with deadly precision. "I see you've been practicing."

Al Mansi nodded, a shy smile crossing his lips. "Yes, I want to be the best I can be," he replied, his Arabic accent thick.

Sarah Lance, also known as the White Canary, walked over to them, her long blonde hair swinging behind her. "We were just talking about your progress," she said as she joined them. "It's remarkable. I don't think I've ever seen anyone advance so quickly."

Al Mansi felt a surge of pride at her words. He had always pushed himself to be better, to prove himself, and it seemed that all his hard work was paying off. "Thank you," he said, feeling his confidence growing.

Nyssa stepped forward, her eyes scanning his weapon. "I have an idea," she said, a mischievous glint in her eyes. "Why don't we train together? I could show you how to use the bow and arrow more effectively, and you could teach me about your meteor hammer."

Al Mansi's eyes widened in surprise at her suggestion. Nyssa was one of the most skilled fighters he had ever seen, and the idea of training with her was both exciting and intimidating. "I would be honored," he said, bowing slightly.

Sarah grinned. "Sounds like a plan to me," she said, clapping her hands together. "Let's get started."

And so they began their training session, with Nyssa and Al Mansi working on long-range weapons while Sarah watched and offered advice. Al Mansi was amazed at the way Nyssa moved, her arrows flying straight and true, while she seemed to be even more impressed with his proficiency with the meteor hammer.

As the session went on, Al Mansi found himself growing more comfortable with his two fellow assassins. They had a camaraderie that he had never experienced before, and he found himself enjoying their company more than he had thought possible. 

By the end of the day, they had made significant progress, and Nyssa and Sarah both had a newfound respect for Al Mansi's abilities. They parted ways, promising to train together again soon, and Al Mansi found himself smiling as he made his way back to his room.

He knew that the road ahead was long and difficult, but with friends like Nyssa and Sarah by his side, he felt that anything was possible.

Month passed and they became a team. As time passes, their team became the stuff of legends even by league standards. The name given to their team was demonstrating this. They were The Three Shadow. Gaining the notice of Ras AlGhul who, with his more than keen eyes, realized that the three were dancing around each other. Yet their combined skills couldn’t be ignored. So he remained silent on the matter. As long as those three knews that their loyalty to the league was most important, he would allow it. No love declaration between Nyssa, Sarah & Al Mansi.

The Three Shadow continued to train together, their skills sharpening with each passing day. They became a formidable team, feared by their enemies and respected by their allies.

Despite their close bond, there was never any romantic interest between Nyssa, Sarah, and Al Mansi. They were too focused on their training and their dedication to the League to let anything like that get in the way.

Ras Al Ghul had been watching them carefully, observing their movements and their interactions with one another. He saw the way they worked seamlessly together, their strengths complementing each other perfectly. He knew that The Three Shadow had the potential to become the most powerful team the League had ever seen.

But he also saw the way they danced around each other, their feelings carefully hidden beneath the surface. Ras Al Ghul knew that such distractions could be dangerous, but he also knew that The Three Shadow were too valuable to lose. So he remained silent on the matter, trusting that their loyalty to the League would always come first.

Months turned into years, and The Three Shadow became the stuff of legends. No one could match their skills in combat, and their reputation spread far and wide. They continued to train together, always pushing each other to be better, and their bond grew stronger with each passing day.

As Ras Al Ghul watched them from afar, he knew that The Three Shadow would one day have to face the greatest challenge of all. But he also knew that they were ready for it, that their strength as a team was greater than any individual could ever hope to achieve. And so he waited, content in the knowledge that The Three Shadow would be ready for whatever the future held.

Set juste after the end of Season 1. As years passed, Al Mansi presence helped Sarah not lost herself in the killing. And when news of the undertakings, of the Glades of Starling City reach them, he offered support. « Maybe I could go there in your place. Make sure your loved one there are self and learn how it was possible that someone such as Al Saher could betray us without anyone noticing. It could be useful in the futur too. Nysa what do you think your father will say about it ? ». In the end, Ras Al Ghul agreed for Al Mansi to go. But without Sarah nor Nyssa. Sarah was too much emotionally involved & Nyssa needed to be there for the time being. Al Mansi mission was to learn how Malcom Merlyn could have done that without being notice by the league, who was the warrior who had managed to defeat him & learn more of this man. On a more personal note, he would make sure that Sarah family was still there.

Al Mansi had long admired Sarah for her unrelenting dedication to the League's mission, but he also knew that the constant killing could take a toll on even the strongest of warriors. When news of the Glades in Starling City reached their ears, he saw the toll it took on her.

He offered his support to Sarah, knowing that he could go in her place and ensure that her loved ones were safe. He also saw an opportunity to learn more about the man who had managed to deceive the League and take down its former leader.

Nyssa was hesitant at first, knowing that her father would not easily agree to such a plan. But Al Mansi's dedication to the League and his valuable skills could not be ignored. In the end, Ras Al Ghul agreed to allow Al Mansi to travel to Starling City, but without Sarah or Nyssa.

Al Mansi set out on his mission, his eyes scanning the city as he searched for any clues as to how Malcolm Merlyn had managed to deceive the League. He spoke with locals, gathered information, and kept a watchful eye on those who might pose a threat to Sarah's loved ones.

As he worked, he also kept a watchful eye on Sarah's family, making sure they were safe and secure. He knew that the emotional toll of the mission would be too much for Sarah to handle, and he did everything in his power to ensure that her loved ones were taken care of.

And as he delved deeper into the mystery of the Glades, he began to sense that there was more at play than just one man's betrayal. He saw the darkness that lurked at the heart of the city, and he knew that the League would need to keep a watchful eye on it in the years to come.

In the end, Al Mansi returned to the League with valuable information about Malcolm Merlyn and the city of Starling City. He knew that Sarah and Nyssa would be proud of the work he had done, and he felt a deep sense of satisfaction in knowing that he had played a small part in protecting the League from its enemies.

He was there, one evening, to protect Laurel Lance from thugs "im juste repaying a debt to a friend" he said before disappearing into the night. He was also there, longer than he would have like, to help The Hood against the centrifuge thief. His presence was already making wave.

Al Mansi had always been dedicated to his training and his duty to the League, but his time in Starling City had shown him the importance of being there for those who needed him, even if it was just repaying a debt to a friend.

One evening, while walking the streets of Starling City, he saw Laurel Lance being attacked by a group of thugs. Without hesitation, he sprang into action, easily dispatching the attackers and ensuring that Laurel was safe.

As he disappeared into the night, he knew that his actions would not go unnoticed. But he also knew that it was the right thing to do, and he could not turn a blind eye to someone in need.

His presence in the city was already making waves, and he found himself drawn into the conflict between The Hood and the centrifuge thief. He had not planned on getting involved, but his skills were needed, and he could not let his teammates fight alone.

As he fought alongside The Hood, he felt a sense of camaraderie that he had not felt since his time with The Three Shadow. Together, they were a force to be reckoned with, taking down the thief and ensuring that the centrifuge was returned to safety.

In the aftermath of the battle, Al Mansi realized that his time in Starling City may not be over yet. There was darkness lurking in the city, and he felt an obligation to protect those who could not protect themselves.

He knew that his dedication to the League would always come first, but he also knew that he could make a difference in this city, that he could be a light in the darkness. And so he continued to watch over Starling City, ready to help those in need and stand against the forces of evil.

As the weekds passed, it was Al Mansi who stopped a mirakuru-enraged Roy Harper to kiss a cop, holding him until The Arrow made his appearance. For once in his life, Al Mansi was notice. And he didnt like this feeling when coming from others than Sara & Nyssa.

Al Mansi had always preferred to work in the shadows, his skills unnoticed by anyone except his teammates in The Three Shadow. But as he continued to help protect the people of Starling City, his actions began to draw the attention of others.

One night, he found himself in the middle of a confrontation with a mirakuru-enraged Roy Harper. Al Mansi sprang into action, using his skills to hold Roy back until The Arrow could arrive and calm him down.

At that moment, Al Mansi realized that he was being noticed by the people of Starling City. It was an uncomfortable feeling for him, knowing that his actions were being watched and judged by others. He had always preferred to work quietly, getting the job done without any fanfare.

But as he thought about it more, he realized that this attention could also be a good thing. If people knew that there were those working to protect them, perhaps they would be more aware of the danger that surrounded them.

And so Al Mansi continued to work in the shadows, his dedication to the League unwavering. But now he also knew that his actions could have an impact on those around him, and he was prepared to do what was necessary to keep the people of Starling City safe.

As the weeks turned into months, Al Mansi continued to help protect the people of Starling City. He fought alongside The Arrow and his team, honing his skills and working to make a difference in the city.

He was still uncomfortable with the attention that came with his actions, but he knew that it was a necessary part of the job. People needed to know that they were safe, and it was up to him and his teammates to ensure that they were.

Over time, he found himself becoming more and more invested in the city and its people. He saw the poverty and the crime that plagued the streets, and he knew that there was much work to be done.

But he also saw the hope in the eyes of those who lived there, the way they faced each day with resilience and determination. And he felt a strong sense of pride in knowing that he was doing his part to protect them.

With each passing day, Al Mansi grew more confident in his abilities and his role in the fight against evil. He knew that there would be challenges in the days ahead, but he also knew that he had the strength and the skill to overcome them.

And so he continued to work in the shadows, a silent protector of the city that he had come to love. He knew that his actions could make a difference, and he was determined to ensure that the people of Starling City had a chance to live in safety and peace.
In his more civil persona, he found himself talking to the sister of his beloved Sara. Laurel was not quite what he had imagined her to be.

Al Mansi had always had a certain image of Laurel Lance in his mind, the sister of the woman he had loved. But as he spoke with her, he realized that she was not quite what he had expected.

Laurel was strong-willed and determined, with a fire in her that reminded him of Sara. But there was also a vulnerability to her, a sadness that lingered just beneath the surface.

As he talked with her, Al Mansi couldn't help but feel a sense of connection. They had both lost someone they loved, and they both carried a burden of responsibility for the safety of their city.

He found himself drawn to Laurel's passion and her fierce dedication to justice. He saw the potential for greatness in her, and he knew that she had the strength to carry on despite her pain.

Over time, their conversations became more frequent, and Al Mansi found himself opening up to Laurel in a way that he had never done before. She listened to him, offering support and understanding when he needed it most.

And as they talked, Al Mansi began to feel as though he had found a kindred spirit in Laurel Lance. Their shared experiences had brought them together, and he knew that he would always be grateful for the bond that they had formed.
Over time, Al Mansi was made aware of Malcom Merlyn continued survival & started looking for him.

As time went on, Al Mansi couldn't shake the feeling that Malcolm Merlyn's continued survival was a threat to the safety of the League and the people of Starling City. He knew that he had to do something about it.

Using his skills and experience, Al Mansi delved into the shadows, searching for any clue as to Malcolm Merlyn's whereabouts. He spoke with informants and went undercover, determined to find the one who had betrayed the League.

As he worked, he also reached out to his teammates in The Three Shadow, hoping to gather more information and support in his search. He knew that he couldn't do this alone, and he was willing to risk everything to bring Malcolm Merlyn to justice.

Over time, his efforts paid off, and he finally received a lead on Malcolm's location. Al Mansi moved quickly, using all of his skills to track down the former League member.

Finally, he found Malcolm Merlyn, and a fierce battle ensued. It was an intense fight, with both men using all of their skill and experience to gain the upper hand.

In the end, Al Mansi emerged victorious, and Malcolm Merlyn was taken into custody. The League was grateful for Al Mansi's dedication and determination, and the people of Starling City were safer for it.

As he looked back on his journey, Al Mansi knew that it had been a difficult one, but it had also been worth it. He had protected the people he loved and had upheld the honor of the League. And he knew that he would always be there to fight for what was right, no matter the cost.

In his somewhat civil persona, life seem to be as boring as he could have expected for someone with his level of skills & capacity to play the relation game. He somehow somewhat, without really knowing how, found himself talking with Quentin Lance about the undertaking. One thing was sure, the man was struggling but trying to come back to himself.

As Al Mansi continued to live his life in the shadows, he found that his civil persona was often at odds with the skills and experience he had gained as an assassin. But he also knew that it was important to maintain a certain level of normalcy, to be able to blend in with the people around him.

One day, he found himself talking with Quentin Lance, the former detective who had been struggling since the Undertaking. Al Mansi couldn't help but feel a sense of empathy towards the man, knowing that he too had lost loved ones in his fight against evil.

As they talked, Al Mansi saw the pain and the struggle in Quentin's eyes. But he also saw a glimmer of hope, a determination to come back from the darkness that threatened to consume him.

Al Mansi offered words of support and encouragement, using his own experiences to help guide Quentin through his own struggles. He saw the potential in the man, the strength and resilience that lay beneath the pain.

Over time, their talks became more frequent, and Al Mansi found himself forging a bond with Quentin Lance. He saw the man as a kindred spirit, someone who had been through the fire and had emerged stronger for it.

And as he looked back on his own journey, Al Mansi knew that these moments of connection and friendship were just as important as any battle he had fought. For in these moments, he found a sense of purpose and a reason to keep fighting, even when the darkness threatened to consume him.
